2010 http://www2.eecs.berkeley.edu/Pubs/TechRpts/2010/EECS-2010-76.pdf DASH+Wings is a small hexapedal winged robot derived from the Dynamic Autonomous Sprawled Hexapod (DASH). Drawing inspiration from nature, DASH+Wings uses wing assisted running t

om nature, DASH+Wings uses wing assisted running to enable increased mobility. It shows a great improvement over DASH in its ability to traverse smooth inclines. To understand the differences in the motion of DASH and DASH+Wings, they are fitted wi

motion of DASH and DASH+Wings, they are fitted with the GINA mote, a small electronics package that contains a processor, IMU, and radio. The acceleration, velocity and position profile as the robots are running can be compared, and the differences

s are running can be compared, and the differences in motion quantified. The accelerometer data can also be used to classify good versus poor running performance, and assist with proper tuning of the system. Advisors: Ronald S. Fearing BibTeX citati
